start.speaker: A Voice on the Wind
start.text: Pilgrim... the fields you knew are still green. Your chair is still warm by the fire. Why wear out your one life chasing a rumour of light?
start.text_zh: 朝圣者啊……你熟悉的田野依旧青翠，炉火旁你的椅子还是暖的。何苦耗尽你这一生，去追一个关于光的传闻？

choices.text: What I left was good — but it was not life. I have set my face to the road.
choices.text_zh: 我所离开的是美好的——但那不是生命。我已定意面向这条路。

choices.text: I do miss it. God help me, I miss it — but missing is not the same as turning back.
choices.text_zh: 我的确想念它。神帮助我，我想念它——但想念，并不等于回头。

choices.text: Surely one look back can do no harm...
choices.text_zh: 回头看一眼，总归不会有什么害处吧……

silenced.text: You do not answer it again. The wind thins, and the voice with it, until there is only the sound of your own steady steps.
silenced.text_zh: 你不再回应它。风渐渐稀薄，那声音也随之消散，最后只剩下你自己沉稳的脚步声。

ache.text: The ache is real, and you let it be real. You carry it forward instead of turning back for it — and that, too, is faith.
ache.text_zh: 那酸楚是真实的，你也任它真实。你带着它向前，而不为它回头——这，也是信心。

tempted.text: You glance over your shoulder. The far glow of the city tugs at you — and you force yourself to face forward again before your feet can follow your eyes.
tempted.text_zh: 你回头望了一眼。那城远远的火光揪着你——你强迫自己重新面向前方，免得脚跟着眼睛走了。
